WorkOrder.title: WorkOrder
WorkOrder.description: Represents a work order in the system based on the Small Job Form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkOrder entity (Job #).

createdDate.description: Date when the work order was created.

billTo.description: The name of the client to bill.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client to bill for the job.

jobName.description: Name of the job, derived from the selected WorkSite.

workSiteId.description: Reference to the WorkSite where the order is located.

description.description: Detailed description of the work order.

quotedAmount.description: The quoted amount for the job.

timeAndMaterial.description: Is the job billed based on time and material.

exempt.description: Is the job exempt from tax.

permitFiled.description: Date when the permit was filed.

coi.description: Is a Certificate of Insurance required.

bondSent.description: Date when the bond was sent.

certifiedPayrollRequested.description: Date when certified payroll was requested.

estimator.description: Name of the estimator or requester.

status.description: Current status of the work order (e.g., Open, Assigned, In Progress, Completed).

tempOnArrival.description: Temperature recorded upon arrival at the job site.

tempOnLeaving.description: Temperature recorded upon leaving the job site.

customerSignatureUrl.description: URL of the captured customer signature image.

signatureDate.description: The date the signature was captured.

Technician.title: Technician
Technician.description: Represents a technician in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Technician entity.

roleId.description: Reference to the role of the technician.

disabled.description: Indicates if the technician's account is disabled.

WorkOrderUpdate.title: WorkOrderUpdate
WorkOrderUpdate.description: Represents an update to a work order, including notes and photos.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkOrderUpdate entity.

notes.description: Notes about the work order update. This serves as the description for any attached photos.

photoUrls.description: URLs of photos related to the work order update.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account within the application.

passwordHash.description: Hashed password for authentication.  Important: Store only the hash, never the plain text password.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Represents a role within the application, defining access permissions.

name.description: Name of the role (e.g., Administrator, Technician, Read-Only).

permissions.description: List of permissions associated with the role.

WorkSite.title: WorkSite
WorkSite.description: Represents a physical location where work is performed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkSite entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the work site (e.g., 'Main Office Building A').

address.description: The full street address of the work site.

state.description: State or province of the work site.

zip.description: Postal code of the work site.

contact.description: Primary contact person for the work site.

notes.description: Any relevant notes about the location, such as access codes or instructions.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client to be billed for work orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

address.description: The billing address of the client.

state.description: State or province of the client.

contact.description: Primary contact person for the client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TrainingRecord entity.

trainingCourse.description: The name of the training course.

description.description: Description of the course, including belts and filters.

trainerSignatureUrl.description: URL of the captured trainer signature image.

checklist.description: A record of the completed items from the Owners Training Check List.

TimeEntry.title: TimeEntry
TimeEntry.description: Represents a single time entry for a technician.

technicianId.description: Reference to the Technician who logged the time.

workOrderId.description: Reference to the WorkOrder this time is for. Null for non-productive time.

date.description: The date the work was performed.

hours.description: The number of hours worked, in 0.25 increments.

timeType.description: The type of time being logged.

notes.description: Notes for the time entry, especially for non-productive time.

definition.description: Stores user profiles, including email, password hash, and role. Role assignment determines access control.
Defines roles and their associated permissions.
Stores work orders. Includes the denormalized field `assignedTechnicianId` for authorization independence.

params.name: workOrderId
params.description: Unique identifier for the work order.

definition.description: Stores work orders assigned to a specific technician. Includes denormalized `assignedTechnicianId` to avoid `get()` calls in security rules.  Duplicates data from /work_orders/{workOrderId}.

params.name: workOrderId
params.description: Unique identifier for the work order.

definition.description: Stores updates to work orders, including notes and photos. Includes denormalized `technicianId` from the parent `work_order` document for authorization independence.

params.name: workOrderId
params.description: Unique identifier for the work order.

params.name: updateId
params.description: Unique identifier for the work order update.

definition.description: Stores information about physical work locations.

params.name: workSiteId
params.description: Unique identifier for the work site.

definition.description: Stores information about clients for billing.

params.name: recordId
params.description: Unique identifier for the training record.

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for the HVAC Start-Up Report.

params.name: timeEntryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the time entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support a work order tracking application with user roles and technician assignments. It emphasizes authorization independence and secure list operations (QAPs) through denormalization and structural segregation.  User authentication is handled via email/password.

*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores user profiles, including email, password hash, and a reference to their role.
*   `/roles/{roleId}`: Defines roles and their associated permissions.  Users are assigned roles which determine their access control.
*   `/technicians/{technicianId}`: Stores technician profiles.
*   `/work_orders/{workOrderId}`: Stores work orders. The `assignedTechnicianId` field links work orders to technicians. Includes the denormalized field `technicianId`.
*   `/technicians/{technicianId}/work_orders/{workOrderId}`: This subcollection holds work orders assigned to a specific technician.  It improves query performance when a technician needs to list their assigned work orders.  Critically, it duplicates all the `work_orders` data but includes a denormalized `technicianId` to avoid `get()` calls.
*   `/work_orders/{workOrderId}/updates/{updateId}`: Stores updates to work orders, including notes and photos. Includes the denormalized `technicianId`.
*   `/counters/{counterId}`: Stores atomic counters to generate unique sequential IDs for entities like Work Orders. This prevents race conditions and ensures ID uniqueness.
*   `/work_sites/{workSiteId}`: Stores information about physical work locations, which can be linked to work orders.
*   `/clients/{clientId}`: Stores information about clients that can be billed.
*   `/training_records/{recordId}`: Stores training attendance records.
*   `/hvac_startup_reports/{reportId}`: Stores HVAC Start-Up Reports.
*   `/time_entries/{timeEntryId}`: Stores individual time entries for technicians.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**

*   The most crucial aspect of this design is authorization independence. For example, `/work_orders/{workOrderId}/updates/{updateId}` denormalizes the `technicianId`. This removes the need to perform `get()` operations in security rules to check the technician's role or ownership of the parent `work_order` document.  The rules can directly validate if `request.auth.uid == resource.data.technicianId`.
*   Similarly, the `/technicians/{technicianId}/work_orders` collection also denormalizes `technicianId` to enable authorization without relying on `get()` calls.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The structure facilitates secure `list` operations. For instance, a technician can securely list work orders assigned to them by querying `/technicians/{technicianId}/work_orders`.  The security rules will only allow access to work orders where `resource.data.technicianId == request.auth.uid`.
*   Roles are implemented using simple document existence checks in `/roles/{roleId}`, which allows efficient QAPs.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):**

The roles are stored directly in the database in the `/roles/{roleId}` collection and referenced by `users/{userId}` documents. The existence of role documents is checked to establish authorization.
